title: Vaccinium maderense
intro: Vaccinium maderense, also known as the Madeira blueberry, is a rare and endangered species of shrub native to the island of Madeira in Portugal. The health benefits of Vaccinium maderense include its potential to support cardiovascular health, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties, making it a valuable addition to a balanced diet. The bioactive constituents of this plant include anthocyanins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Vaccinium maderense are capsules and teas, although it is also used in the production of jams, jellies, and other food products.
taxonomy: Vaccinium maderense is a species of flowering plant in the family Ericaceae. It is native to the island of Madeira in Portugal. The plant is classified as a member of the Vaccinium genus, which includes blueberries and cranberries. Vaccinium maderense is further classified as a member of the section Cyanococcus, which is a group of small to medium-sized shrubs. The species is also known as the Madeira blueberry.
